rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DELORES HEARRING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/16/2024 for the following remaining issues to be addressed: 1) A copy of letters issued to the personal representative of Margaret Johnson's estate and certified within 60 days must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. See LRSF 14.35(G) & 14.59(C)(6). 2) The address of the petitioner's former attorney listed on the proof of service does not appear current. 3) If the petitioner's former attorney has not waived partial statutory compensation, and if there is no apportionment agreement between both attorneys, sufficient information must be provided as to the services actually rendered by each attorney, as required by CRC 7.704(a) & Probate Code section 10814, in support of a proposed allocation.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARGUERITE ROBERTA WEBER FBO TERRY PARISH TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the courtesy copy of the petition received by the examiner had attached to the first page the pleading for the Richard Parish subtrust, including that subtrust's accounting, not the accounting for this subtrust. It would be helpful, if accountings continue to be filed with the Court, if the successor trustee provided a copy of order creating the testamentary trust. It is unclear when or under what conditions these subtrusts terminate; they do not appear to benefit the beneficiaries any longer.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIE HALL PINCHIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/1/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) In the [second] supplement, filed on 12/18/23, the calculation of the net distribution amounts appears to be incorrect. If there is $281,639.67 on hand (as of 8/31/23) and statutory compensation is $7,256.97 each to the attorney and the administrator, and $3,000 is to be set aside as a reserve to prepare and file the final tax return for the estate, the net amount should be $264,125.73. A proposed order and courtesy copies of an AMENDMENT to the petition, showing the correct calculation of the net distributable amounts, and proof of service of notice of the amendment must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/22/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Please do not make the amendment to the petition an "answer" to these issues. It should stand on its own and simply show the correct calculation, not recite these issues. NB: In order for  supplements, amendments, or fully amended pleadings  to be reviewed prior to a continued hearing the filing party must deliver courtesy copies to the Examiners  with the SCHEDULED HEARING DATE NOTED ON THE FACE SHEET AND ANY COVER LETTER. [LRSR 14.7 B.])

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE STEWART LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The Court is to determine whether to approve section 6.3(b) of the reconstructed trust (see Exhibit A to the supplement to the subject petition filed by Norman Stewart Jr.) purporting to designate Barbara Stewart as the sole beneficiary of the entire Egbert Ave property (1225-1235 Egbert Avenue). According to Norman Stewart Jr.'s declaration previously filed (see Exhibit B to the first amended petition filed in this trust case on 02/22/2021 - see also Attachment 3f(3) to the amended petition for probate of Norman Stewart Sr.'s lost will filed on 07/16/2020 in the related case PES-20-303558), Sonja Stewart and Tanis Kidd were the designated beneficiaries entitled to receive a portion of the Egbert Ave property (1235 Egbert Avenue), and Barbara Stewart was the designated beneficiary only entitled to receive the remaining portion of the same property (1225 Egbert Avenue). 2) The Court is to determine whether to approve section 6.3(b) of the reconstructed trust (see Exhibit A to the supplement to the subject petition filed by Norman Stewart Jr.) purporting to authorize direct distribution of the Egbert Ave property to Barbara Stewart's 3 children: Tanis Kidd; Sonja Stewart; and Treasure Clark, in the event Barbara Stewart failed to survive final distribution of the Egbert Ave property, even if Barbara Stewart survived both settlors. According to Norman Stewart Jr.'s declaration (see Exhibit K to the subject petition filed by Norman Stewart Jr.), if any child of the settlors predeceased them, the child's trust share was to go to the predeceased child's surviving issue. Barbara Stewart, one of the settlors' children, survived both settlors, but died before final distribution of 1225 Egbert Ave from the trust. If her interest in 1225 Egbert Ave vested once she survived both settlors, Barbara Stewart's probate (PES-20-303559) must be reopened for her estate to receive 1225 Egbert Ave from the trust. Barbara Stewart's estate must then distribute 1225 Egbert Ave to her 3 children and heirs (Tanis Kidd, Sonja Stewart, and Treasure Clark), subject to each child/heir's reallocation/buy-out rights and obligations under the 2019 settlement agreement if it remains in effect and executory. 3) The Court is to determine whether each and every term of the reconstructed trust (see Exhibit A to the supplement to the subject petition filed by Norman Stewart Jr.) was established by sufficient secondary evidence. See Dart Industries, Inc. v. Commercial Union Ins. Co. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 1059, 1068-1070; see also Evidence Code sections 1521 & 1523. If not so established, the Court is to determine whether to limit its reconstruction of the trust terms and only confirm the essential elements of creating the trust, such as the identities of both settlors, date of their trust, successor trustees (section 7.1), and beneficiaries entitled to receive each subdivided portion of the Lyon St. property and Egbert Ave property (section 6.3(a) & (b)). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BARBARA A. LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/17/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 02/27/2024) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petition must be verified by the executor. 4) All the information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1) must be provided. Allegations must be made as to notice pursuant to Probate Code section 9202 and any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution. 5) The petition must be amended to request a specific period for which a continuance of administration is sought. 6) No final inventory and appraisal has been filed. 7) The decedent died more than 2 years ago, but no first interim account has been filed. Waivers of the first account signed by beneficiaries of the devisee trust have been filed, but the trustee must waive the account. The trustee's waiver must include language required by Probate Code section 10954(b)(4).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WYN WANG LEUNG IRREVOCABLE TRUST DATED APRIL 20, TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petition includes not only the trust accounting, but also an accounting of non-trust assets such as the settlor's IRA and other retirement accounts and report of the petitioner's acts taken as attorney-in-fact and agent under the settlor's power of attorney. The petitioner has not opened a new case (PES) under the name of the power of attorney, nor has the petitioner filed a separate POA petition under section 4541(b), to seek approval of her accounting with respect to the non-trust assets covered by the power of attorney, rather than the trust, despite the Court's directive so given to the petitioner's counsel at the last hearing on 01/16/2024. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARET A. FOOTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/1/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Now a Second Amended Petition has been filed. The notice shows a "Second Corrected Amended Notice of Petition to Administer Estate," which is not the title of this pleading and the notice does not have "Second Amended" Petition interlineated in item 2, to clearly show what pleading is to be heard. (2) Neither the mailed nor published notice indicate that a Second Amended Petition has been filed, seeking to admit a LOST will and codicil. [LRSF 14.20.] (3) Item 2b of the second amended petition is blank. (4) Item 3f(2) of the petition also does not have "LOST" interlineated before "will" and "codicil." (5) William W. Foote is missing from item 8 of this petition. (6) The second Attachment 3f(3) continues item 8 of the second amended petition. (7) Paragraph 4 of the first Attachment 3f(3) appears to be incomplete. (8) The will mentions two possible devisee trusts. Is the petitioner the trustee of both trusts? Deadline is 4/22/24 for providing courtesy copies to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. ("Succ. executor & trustee" is not a relationship to the decedent; one is either kin or a stranger.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DELORISA TURNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed: 1) No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Form DE-147S) has been filed (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.E). 2) Item 3h(2) of the petition indicates Jessica Esmay is an out of state resident, but the permanent address indicated for Ms. Esmay is her attorney's address in Oakland. Please indicate petitioner's permanent out of state address. 3) Bond is required for out of state residents. (San Francisco Local Rule 14.16.F) Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DELORISA TURNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed: 1) A competing petition was filed by an individual with higher priority under Probate Code Section 8461. 2) Item 3f(1) of the petition indicates that decedent died intestate, but decedent's will dated December 31, 2018 has been lodged with the court. 3) Item 5a(6) of the petition indicates that decedent was survived by no child, which is inconsistent with the information provided in the competing petition. 4) Petitioner's name is not listed in Item 8. 5) No Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (Form DE-121) has been filed (Probate Code Section 8100). 6) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GEORGE LA BAR TRUST DATED JANUARY 15, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition: 1. Undue Influence 2. To Invalidate Trust; 3. For Mistake; 4. For Fraud;, 5. For Financial Elder Abuse; 6. To Compel Accounting Of Attorney-
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The notice filed on 2/22/24 does not indicate what was filed (Item 1 refers to an attachment, but no attachment was attached). 2) The notice filed on 2/22/24 does not specifically identify the account numbers for financial accounts. 3) Mary Scanlan was named as an individual, in addition to as a trustee; however, she has not been personally served. 4) Proposed order has not been submitted. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESTHER HAEJI JUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/7/2024. The petition is premature for the examiner's review as all required documents have not been filed: including but not limited to (1) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate [DE-121] for proof of proper notice. Prob. Code sec. 8100; 8110. (2) Proof of publication. Prob. Code 8120 et seq. (3) Duties and Liabilities of Personal Rep [Form DE-147] (4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [Form DE-147S] to be conventionally filed. See the Local Rule of SF 14.18.E and 14.59.C. (5) Proposed Order for Probate [DE-140] to be submitted. Local Rule 14.7.A (6) Further allegation of why this petition is necessary. (7) The caption of the petition needs to set forth the Full IAEA authority being sought as Petition #2.C indicates so. These issues must be cleared two weeks before the next hearing for the examiner's review.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DONALD S. TSAO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/1/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The will is not self-proving. The witnesses did not swear under penalty of perjury as to their attestation. (2) Notice must have attached to it the instructions for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (4) Item 4 of the petition was not checked. (5) Item 8 of the petition does not show the relationship of each person listed. (6) If an heir predeceased, the petition must clearly indicate the date and whether or not she was survived by issue. (7) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Who are or were the parents of the niblings listed in item 8 of the petition? (8) Who was the predeceased wife and when did she die? (9) Other siblings named in the will are missing from item 8 of the petition. (10) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] Please provide courtesy copies to the assigned examiner by 4/22/24. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The will waived bond for the executor only.)
